message:
test(storage/fts): expand FTS5 injection corpus from 2 to 12 grammar-aware payloads (ft-9ypcc)

Fresh-eyes review of 9845e98e flagged the original injection harness as
thin: only two payloads (`" OR 1 --` and `unknowncol:needle`). The
commit message claimed it "pins FTS5 injection-surface behavior" but the
actual surface — NEAR operator, real column prefixes, quote balance,
Unicode tokenizer, prefix wildcards, parenthesis nesting, empty input,
oversized payloads — was unprobed.

Add a structure-aware fixture set covering every FTS5 grammar production
the renderer / MCP envelope can plausibly receive:

* `near_operator_well_formed_payload_succeeds` — NEAR(a b, n),
  NEAR("a" "b", 10), NEAR with default distance.
* `near_operator_malformed_payload_stays_in_structured_fts_error_path`
  — non-int distance, missing token list, trailing comma. Note:
  bare `NEAR alpha beta` is NOT malformed; FTS5 only reserves NEAR
  inside the parenthesized form.
* `real_content_column_prefix_is_accepted_as_valid_fts_syntax` — pins
  that `content:needle` works (content is the only column on
  output_segments_fts per storage.rs:187-192). Catches a future schema
  rename that would silently break column-qualified queries.
* `pane_id_column_ref_is_rejected_because_fts_table_only_has_content`
  — pane_id lives on the base table, not the FTS5 virtual table; must
  return the structured "no such column" error.
* `unbalanced_quote_payloads_stay_in_structured_fts_error_path`.
* `unicode_tokenizer_edges_succeed_without_widening_to_sql` — café,
  日本語, 🎉, zero-width space, combining diacritics, mirrored ASCII.
* `prefix_match_abuse_is_handled_or_rejected_consistently` — trailing
  star valid, leading/bare star rejected, all stay in-band.
* `parenthesis_nesting_payloads_stay_in_grammar` — 11-deep balanced
  succeeds; lopsided / unbalanced lands on structured error path.
* `empty_and_whitespace_only_payloads_resolve_without_panic` — empty
  string, whitespace, bare AND/OR/NOT keywords.
* `very_long_payload_does_not_blow_the_validation_path` — 8 KiB
  payload (1024 token repeats + 256-term OR chain).

A new `assert_injection_outcome` helper enforces the binary contract on
every payload: outcome is either Success or StructuredFtsError; any
other variant (panic, raw rusqlite error, anyhow chain) fails the test
loudly. Keeps per-payload assertions terse and prevents drift.

Verified via:

  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target \
    CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \
    CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test search_fts_query_injection

12 tests pass (the original 2 + 10 new). No production change.
